title: NOC 41405 – Education Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Education Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers under NOC 41405 are professionals who design, evaluate, and implement educational policies and curriculum frameworks across Canada. Classified under TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role supports the development of education systems, academic standards, curriculum innovation, and institutional program effectiveness. These professionals work within government ministries of education, school boards, universities, research institutes, consulting organizations, and professional associations.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41405

duties: Education Policy Researchers and Program Officers may:
Conduct research and prepare policy reports
Administer education policies and academic programs
Develop structure, objectives, and content of new programs
Conduct cost-effectiveness analyses of education policies
Develop instructional materials and program resources
Deliver presentations at conferences and workshops
Supervise policy researchers and program officers
Administer specialized testing and assessment programs
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in:EducationSocial sciencesBusiness administrationRelated discipline
Master’s degree in education may be required
Several years of teaching experience usually required
Teacher’s certificate often required when employed by provincial education ministries
Specialized certification may be required depending on role
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Education Policy Researchers and Program Officers typically earn between:
$45,400 – $104,900 per year,depending on experience, specialization, and region.
Senior education consultants and policy directors may earn higher compensation.
With 18,500 projected job openings, NOC 41405 demonstrates steady employment potential due to:
Increased focus on policy evaluation and accountability
Education reform remains a priority across provinces and territories.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 41405 are employed in:
Stable employment in public sector institutions
Eligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs
Opportunities to influence national and provincial education systems
Professionals with teaching experience, curriculum development expertise, and policy analysis backgrounds are particularly competitive.
pathways: Professionals under this NOC may qualify for:
Advanced degrees and Canadian-recognized teaching credentials strengthen immigration competitiveness.
